hypothesis: If an LLM-based agent framework equipped with structured external tools (memory, periodic table, and domain knowledge base) iteratively proposes and refines inorganic material compositions using property feedback, then it will achieve higher success rates in targeting specific formation energies and generate more valid, unique, and novel compositions compared to tool-free LLM baselines and diffusion-model-only approaches, because the iterative retrieval-augmented reasoning enables the agent to systematically narrow the compositional search space guided by chemical knowledge and prior experimental outcomes.
